Summary of what was found: Amcor has a recent supplier-controlled paper-flex family for high-paper-content packaging film with thin polymer film, barrier material, and patterned sealant intended to keep hermetic seals while minimizing non-fiber content and thickness.
Detailed patent mapping: Publication family: EP4284635B1. Assignee: Amcor Flexibles North America Inc. Priority year: 2021. Filing year: 2022. Publication year: 2023 application publication and 2024 grant. Jurisdiction: EP. Legal status: active per Google Patents. Component: paper-based flexible film for hermetic packs. Material route: paper component plus 2-10 µm polymeric film plus barrier layer plus patterned sealable material. Claimed invention: paper packaging film engineered for high barrier and seal integrity, including triple-point seals, with minimal non-paper thickness. Problem solved: hermetic sealing and seal robustness in paper-rich flexible packs without fully plastic film. Cosmetic relevance: transfer potential for sachets, sheet-mask pouches, wipes overwraps, refill-pouch outer webs, and small-format personal-care packs; weaker for aggressive oily/alcoholic formulas without validation. Premium vs mass-market fit: strongest mass-market and masstige sample/refill formats; limited premium except hidden refill packs. Mapped topics: S13, S17, S20. IP risk signal: High patent density / FTO review recommended; supplier-owned route. White-space note: lower-barrier cosmetic sample packs, dry-product sachets, and secondary overwraps likely have more space than high-barrier direct-contact liquid flex. Caution: older priority included because EP grant was post-2022; Google legal status is informational only.
Source name: Paper packaging with thin film barrier — Google Patents — https://patents.google.com/patent/EP4284635B1/en

Summary of what was found: Nestlé has a recent paper-rich flexible family using paper, aluminum, nanoclay barrier coating, and sealing layer while omitting PE/PP layers, showing active claiming around paper-rich foil-lite or polyolefin-avoiding flexible laminates.

Problem solved: strong O2/WVTR performance in paper-rich flex structures without conventional PE/PP-rich multilayer. Premium vs mass-market fit: better premium/masstige and specialty sample-pack fit than high-volume mass personal care due to complexity and cost. Mapped topics: S13, S17, S20. Caution: older priority included due to post-2022 publication; structure remains multilayer, not simple paper route.
Source name: A multi-layer flexible packaging material — Google Patents — https://patents.google.com/patent/US20240270465A1/en

Summary of what was found: Landscape synthesis: recent paper-based cosmetic lightweighting patents split into paper-rich flexible laminates, coated cartonboard, oxygen/moisture-barrier paper chemistry, vapor-deposited/inorganic barrier papers, and bio-based fiber laminates.
Detailed patent mapping: Consolidated mapping note. Most crowded clusters: Tetra barrier-coated cellulose laminate families, Amcor paper-flex high-barrier sealable film, and P&G/Nestlé paper-laminate sachet/pouch families. Supplier-controlled zones: WestRock aqueous/high-barrier cellulosics, Billerud flow-wrap and heat-sealable paper, Ahlstrom oxygen/moisture barrier papers, and Sappi high-performance barrier papers. Likely white space: lower-barrier cosmetic secondary cartons, paper sleeves and inserts, dry-product sachets, non-direct-contact coated fiber, lower-complexity paperboard with light coatings and recyclable decoration, and premium tactile cartons with modest barrier needs. Higher-caution zones: direct-contact wet/oily/fragranced paper sachets, vapor-deposited or metallized paper laminates, paper-based hermetic refill pouches, and aseptic-style bio-based laminates. Mapped topics: strongest to S17 and S13, then S15 and S19, with S20 for implementation and cost gating. Caution: patent landscape mapping only, not FTO opinion; Google legal-status fields are informational; some families have older priorities but qualifying post-2022 publications/grants.
Source name: Landscape synthesis from cited patent families — Google Patents / WIPO records summarized from rows P04-001 to P04-019
